source.title: Central cancellation of self-produced tickle sensation.

confidence_rationale: High-impact Nature Neuroscience paper establishing that the cerebellum predicts the specific sensory consequences of movement and provides the cancellation signal — the forward model framework underlying SD-007's ReafferencePredictor. fMRI shows reduced somatosensory cortex activity and reduced cerebellar activity for self-produced vs. externally produced touch. The Wolpert group's forward model interpretation (cerebellum as internal model computing sensory prediction) is exactly the computational form implemented in SD-007. Confidence is moderated by modality transfer (tactile -> visual) and by the coarse spatial resolution of fMRI relative to single-unit findings.
failure_signatures: The cancellation is shown to be specific to self-produced stimuli with predictable sensory consequences — externally produced stimuli of identical physical parameters are perceived as significantly more intense. This implies the predictor must be trained on the specific statistical relationship between actions and sensory outcomes; an untrained or poorly calibrated predictor would fail to cancel.

The cerebellum shows less activation during the movement that generates the tactile stimulus than during a movement that does not — suggesting the cancellation signal is computed in relation to the predicted sensory consequence of the specific action, not movement in general.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The cerebellum is involved in predicting the specific sensory consequences of movements, providing the signal used to cancel the sensory response to self-generated stimulation. Self-produced tactile stimuli produce less somatosensory cortex activity than externally produced stimuli.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-007's ReafferencePredictor(z_world_raw_prev, a_prev) is the REE implementation of the cerebellar forward model described here. It takes the prior world state and the motor action as input — just as the cerebellum takes the efference copy of the motor command — and outputs a prediction of the expected change in z_world due to self-movement. This prediction is subtracted to yield the perspective-corrected latent z_world_corrected.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper's mechanism is tactile/somatosensory; SD-007 is about visual perspective correction in a gridworld. The forward-model principle is shared but the specific sensory content differs substantially. Additionally, the cerebellar circuit has a precise biological substrate (granule cells, Purkinje cells, mossy fibres) that the ReafferencePredictor abstracts over.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI, self-produced vs. externally produced tactile stimulation paradigm, healthy adults. Classic paper from the Wolpert/Frith group establishing the cerebellar forward model for sensory cancellation.

confidence_components.notes: Modality transfer (tactile -> visual gridworld) is the primary risk. The forward-model computational claim is strongly supported; the specific neural substrate for visual reafference correction in SD-007's context differs from the somatosensory-cerebellar circuit studied here.
